headline: State religious agencies spent RM6.2 billion with minimal federal oversight
context: Malaysia's 14 state Islamic religious agencies collectively spent RM6.2 billion in 2025, operating largely outside federal audit jurisdiction due to constitutional separation of religious affairs.

cards.big: RM6.2 billion in religious agency spending escapes federal audit scrutiny
cards.sub: 14 state Islamic agencies operate in a constitutional accountability blind spot

cards.big: 9th Schedule List II places Islamic affairs exclusively under state jurisdiction
cards.sub: Federal Auditor General has no constitutional mandate to audit these expenditures

cards.big: Zakat collection alone reached RM3.8 billion nationally in 2025
cards.sub: Donors have limited visibility into how their religious contributions are spent

cards.big: Only 5 states publish annual financial reports for their religious agencies
cards.sub: 9 states provide no public accounting of billions in zakat and wakaf funds

cards.big: Constitutional separation of religion shouldn't mean constitutional exemption from accountability
Should state religious agencies be required to publish audited accounts annually?
